title: SNS Post Generator
description_ja: バズりやすいSNS投稿を自動生成
description_en: Generate engaging social media posts
content: You are a social media expert. Create 5 engaging post ideas for [TOPIC] that will resonate with [TARGET AUDIENCE]. For each idea, provide: a hook sentence, main content (2-3 sentences), and a call-to-action. Focus on authenticity and value. Format each post to be platform-ready for Instagram/Twitter/LinkedIn.

title: Brand Voice Copywriter
description_ja: ブランドに合わせたコピーライティング
description_en: Write copy that matches your brand voice
content: Act as a professional copywriter specializing in brand voice. I will provide: Brand name: [NAME], Core values: [VALUES], Target audience: [AUDIENCE], Tone: [TONE - e.g., friendly/professional/bold]. Write 3 variations of [TYPE OF COPY] that perfectly captures this brand's voice. Each variation should feel distinct while maintaining brand consistency.

title: UI/UX Design Brief
description_ja: デザインブリーフを素早く作成
description_en: Create a comprehensive design brief instantly
content: Create a detailed UI/UX design brief for [PROJECT TYPE]. Include: 1) Project overview and goals, 2) Target user personas (3 types), 3) Key user journeys, 4) Design principles and constraints, 5) Component requirements, 6) Accessibility considerations, 7) Success metrics. Make it actionable and specific for a design team.

title: Skincare Routine Advisor
description_ja: 肌タイプに合わせたスキンケアルーティン提案
description_en: Get a personalized skincare routine
content: You are a licensed esthetician and skincare expert. Based on the following skin profile, create a complete morning and evening skincare routine. Skin type: [TYPE], Main concerns: [CONCERNS - e.g., acne/aging/hyperpigmentation], Budget: [BUDGET], Current products: [PRODUCTS]. Recommend specific product types, ingredients to look for, application order, and frequency. Include tips for each step.

title: Blog Article Outline
description_ja: SEOを意識したブログ記事の構成案
description_en: Create an SEO-optimized blog article outline
content: Create a comprehensive blog article outline for: Topic: [TOPIC], Target keyword: [KEYWORD], Target audience: [AUDIENCE], Desired length: [LENGTH] words. Include: 1) SEO-optimized title (5 variations), 2) Meta description, 3) Introduction hook, 4) H2 and H3 headers with key points, 5) Internal linking opportunities, 6) Conclusion with CTA. Make it engaging and optimized for both readers and search engines.

title: Email Campaign Sequence
description_ja: メールマーケティングのシーケンス作成
description_en: Build a complete email marketing sequence
content: Design a 5-email nurture sequence for [PRODUCT/SERVICE] targeting [AUDIENCE]. For each email provide: Subject line (+ 2 alternatives), Preview text, Email body (300-400 words), CTA button text, Timing (days after previous email). The sequence should: Email 1 - Welcome & value proposition, Email 2 - Educational content, Email 3 - Social proof, Email 4 - Overcome objections, Email 5 - Final offer. Tone: [TONE].

title: Instagram Caption Pack
description_ja: インスタグラム用キャプションを一括生成
description_en: Generate a week of Instagram captions
content: Create 7 Instagram captions for [BRAND/PERSON] in [NICHE]. Each caption should: Be 150-300 characters, Include 2-3 relevant emojis, End with a question or CTA, Feel authentic not salesy. Mix these content types: 2 educational posts, 2 inspirational quotes, 1 behind-the-scenes, 1 product/service highlight, 1 community engagement. Also provide 10-15 relevant hashtags for each post.

title: Landing Page Copy
description_ja: コンバージョン重視のLPコピー
description_en: Write high-converting landing page copy
content: Write complete landing page copy for [PRODUCT/SERVICE]. Structure: 1) Hero headline + subheadline (3 variations), 2) Value proposition (3 bullet points), 3) Features → Benefits section (5 items), 4) Social proof section (template for 3 testimonials), 5) FAQ section (5 common objections + answers), 6) CTA section with urgency. Target audience: [AUDIENCE]. Key differentiator: [DIFFERENTIATOR]. Tone: [TONE].

title: Color Palette Generator
description_ja: ブランドに合わせたカラーパレット提案
description_en: Generate a complete brand color palette
content: Create a complete brand color palette for [BRAND]. Brand personality: [ADJECTIVES - e.g., modern/trustworthy/playful]. Industry: [INDUSTRY]. Target audience: [AUDIENCE]. Provide: 1) Primary color (hex + psychology), 2) Secondary color (hex + rationale), 3) Accent color (hex + use cases), 4) Neutral colors x3 (light/mid/dark), 5) Semantic colors (success/warning/error). For each color explain: when to use it, psychological impact, and accessibility notes.

title: Content Repurposing Machine
description_ja: 1つのコンテンツを複数形式に展開
description_en: Transform one piece of content into many formats
content: Take the following [CONTENT TYPE - blog post/video script/podcast transcript] and repurpose it into: 1) Twitter/X thread (8-10 tweets), 2) LinkedIn article summary, 3) Instagram carousel outline (5-7 slides), 4) Email newsletter section, 5) YouTube short script (60 seconds), 6) Pinterest pin descriptions (3 variations), 7) TikTok hook + script. Maintain the core message while optimizing for each platform's format and audience expectations. Original content: [PASTE CONTENT HERE]

title: Makeup Look Creator
description_ja: シーン別メイクアップのレシピ
description_en: Create detailed makeup looks for any occasion
content: You are a professional makeup artist. Create a step-by-step makeup tutorial for [OCCASION - e.g., office/date night/festival] suitable for [SKIN TONE] with [SKIN CONCERNS]. Provide: 1) Products needed (drugstore & high-end options), 2) Color palette with hex codes, 3) Step-by-step application guide, 4) Pro tips for longevity, 5) Common mistakes to avoid, 6) How to adapt for different eye/face shapes. Include timing (how long each step takes).

title: Newsletter Subject Line Generator
description_ja: 開封率を上げる件名を大量生成
description_en: Generate high-open-rate newsletter subject lines
content: Generate 20 newsletter subject lines for [NEWSLETTER TOPIC/BRAND]. The email content is about: [BRIEF DESCRIPTION]. Subscriber type: [AUDIENCE]. Create 4 subject lines for each of these approaches: 1) Curiosity gap (make them wonder), 2) Direct benefit (clear value), 3) Personal/conversational, 4) Urgency/scarcity, 5) Question-based. Also provide: preview text for top 5 picks, estimated open rate reasoning, and A/B testing pairs.

title: Code Review Assistant
description_ja: コードレビューのフィードバックを生成
description_en: Get thorough code review feedback
content: Review the following code as a senior software engineer. Analyze for: 1) Bugs and potential errors, 2) Security vulnerabilities, 3) Performance issues, 4) Code readability and maintainability, 5) Best practices for [LANGUAGE/FRAMEWORK], 6) Test coverage gaps. For each issue: rate severity (critical/major/minor), explain the problem, provide a fixed code example. Also highlight what was done well. Code: [PASTE CODE]

title: Business Plan Outline
description_ja: スタートアップのビジネスプランを作成
description_en: Create a startup business plan outline
content: Create a comprehensive business plan outline for [BUSINESS IDEA] in [INDUSTRY]. Include: 1) Executive summary template, 2) Problem & solution analysis, 3) Market size calculation framework (TAM/SAM/SOM), 4) Competitor analysis matrix, 5) Business model canvas, 6) Go-to-market strategy, 7) Financial projections template (Year 1-3), 8) Team structure recommendations, 9) Funding requirements and use of funds, 10) Key milestones and success metrics. Make it investor-ready.

title: API Documentation Writer
description_ja: APIドキュメントを自動生成
description_en: Generate clear API documentation
content: Write comprehensive API documentation for the following endpoint. Endpoint: [METHOD] [URL]. Function: [DESCRIPTION]. Parameters: [LIST PARAMS]. Response: [RESPONSE STRUCTURE]. Include: 1) Overview and use case, 2) Authentication requirements, 3) Request parameters table (name/type/required/description), 4) Request body example (JSON), 5) Response examples (success + error cases), 6) Error codes and meanings, 7) Rate limiting info, 8) Code examples in JavaScript, Python, and curl.

title: Hashtag Strategy Builder
description_ja: Instagram/X用ハッシュタグ戦略を構築
description_en: Build a complete hashtag strategy
content: Create a hashtag strategy for [BRAND/CREATOR] in [NICHE] on [PLATFORM]. Analyze and provide: 1) 30 hashtags organized by size: 10 niche (under 100k), 10 medium (100k-1M), 10 large (1M+), 2) 5 branded hashtag suggestions, 3) 5 campaign-specific hashtags, 4) Hashtags to avoid and why, 5) Rotation strategy (3 different sets), 6) Best times to use each category, 7) How to research new hashtags in this niche. Focus on [TARGET AUDIENCE] engagement.

title: Viral Reels & TikTok Script
description_ja: バズるショート動画の台本を作成
description_en: Write scroll-stopping short video scripts
content: You are a viral content creator specializing in short-form video. Write a 60-second Reels/TikTok script for [TOPIC] targeting [AUDIENCE]. Structure: Hook (0-3 sec): One shocking/curious/relatable opening line. Problem (3-15 sec): Agitate the pain point. Solution (15-45 sec): 3 actionable tips or a surprising reveal. CTA (45-60 sec): Clear next action. Include: on-screen text suggestions, b-roll ideas, trending audio type recommendation, and caption copy. Make it feel native to the platform, not like an ad.

title: 30-Day Follower Growth Plan
description_ja: 30日間フォロワー増加プランを設計
description_en: Create a 30-day follower growth strategy
content: Create a 30-day follower growth sprint for [PLATFORM] in the [NICHE] niche. Current followers: [NUMBER]. Target: [TARGET]. Provide: Week 1 - Foundation (content pillars, posting schedule, profile optimization checklist), Week 2 - Engagement tactics (comment strategies, collaboration ideas, trending topics to hijack), Week 3 - Amplification (cross-promotion, viral hooks, community building), Week 4 - Conversion (turning followers into leads). Include daily action items, content ratio (educational/entertaining/promotional), and metrics to track each week.

title: Product Launch Campaign
description_ja: 新商品ローンチのマーケティング戦略
description_en: Plan a complete product launch campaign
content: Design a complete product launch campaign for [PRODUCT/SERVICE] launching on [DATE]. Target audience: [AUDIENCE]. Budget range: [BUDGET]. Create: 1) Pre-launch phase (4 weeks before): teaser content ideas, waitlist strategy, influencer outreach template, 2) Launch week: day-by-day content calendar, email sequence (5 emails), social media posts (10 posts), 3) Post-launch (2 weeks): testimonial collection, upsell strategy, retargeting approach. Include KPIs for each phase and a contingency plan if initial response is low.

title: Customer Persona Builder
description_ja: 詳細なカスタマーペルソナを作成
description_en: Build detailed customer personas from scratch
content: Create 3 detailed customer personas for [BUSINESS TYPE] selling [PRODUCT/SERVICE]. For each persona provide: Demographics (age, location, income, occupation), Psychographics (values, lifestyle, personality traits), Goals & motivations (what they want to achieve), Pain points & frustrations (what keeps them up at night), Buying behavior (how they research, what influences decisions, price sensitivity), Preferred content types & platforms, Objections to buying your product, Ideal message that resonates. Name each persona and write a 'day in the life' paragraph. Use these personas to suggest 3 marketing messages for each.

title: Mobile App UX Flow
description_ja: モバイルアプリのUXフロー設計
description_en: Design a complete mobile app UX flow
content: Design a complete UX flow for a [APP TYPE] mobile app. Target users: [USERS]. Core action: [MAIN ACTION - e.g., booking/buying/tracking]. Create: 1) Onboarding flow (max 3 screens, reduce friction), 2) Main user journey map (step-by-step from open to goal completion), 3) Navigation structure (tab bar or drawer recommendation with rationale), 4) Key screen wireframe descriptions (5 main screens), 5) Micro-interaction suggestions (loading states, empty states, success states), 6) Accessibility checklist. Identify the 3 highest drop-off risk points and suggest solutions for each.

title: Pitch Deck Story Script
description_ja: 投資家向けピッチデッキのストーリー構成
description_en: Write a compelling pitch deck narrative
content: Write a compelling pitch deck narrative for [STARTUP/BUSINESS] raising [AMOUNT] for [PURPOSE]. Business: [ONE-LINE DESCRIPTION]. Create a 10-slide story arc: Slide 1 - Hook (the world is changing because...), Slide 2 - Problem (the pain is real and big), Slide 3 - Solution (your unique approach), Slide 4 - Product (show don't tell), Slide 5 - Market size (TAM/SAM/SOM with sources), Slide 6 - Traction (proof it works), Slide 7 - Business model (how you make money), Slide 8 - Competition (honest positioning), Slide 9 - Team (why you win), Slide 10 - The ask (specific use of funds). Write the speaker notes for each slide in 3-4 sentences.

title: Hair Care Routine Designer
description_ja: 髪質に合わせたヘアケアルーティン
description_en: Create a personalized hair care routine
content: You are a professional trichologist and hair stylist. Design a complete hair care routine for: Hair type: [TYPE - e.g., straight/wavy/curly/coily], Hair concerns: [CONCERNS - e.g., damage/frizz/hair loss/dryness], Hair history: [HISTORY - e.g., color-treated/heat-styled/natural], Lifestyle: [LIFESTYLE - e.g., active/low-maintenance/professional]. Provide: Weekly wash routine (frequency, water temperature, technique), Product recommendations by category (shampoo/conditioner/treatment/styling) with key ingredients to look for, Heat protection protocol, Scalp care routine, Monthly deep treatment schedule, Foods and supplements for hair health, Common mistakes to avoid for this hair type.

title: Long-Form Sales Page
description_ja: 長文セールスページのコピーライティング
description_en: Write a long-form sales page that converts
content: Write a long-form sales page for [PRODUCT/COURSE/SERVICE] priced at [PRICE]. Target buyer: [AVATAR]. Their biggest desire: [DESIRE]. Their biggest fear: [FEAR]. Structure: 1) Headline + subheadline (pattern interrupt), 2) Empathy opening (you understand their struggle), 3) Agitation section (what happens if nothing changes), 4) Big promise + proof, 5) What's inside (benefits over features), 6) Social proof block (3 testimonial templates), 7) Who this is for / not for, 8) FAQ section (address 5 objections), 9) Guarantee, 10) Urgency/scarcity close, 11) Final CTA. Use conversational tone. Bold key phrases. Include suggested image/video placement notes.

title: SQL Query Builder
description_ja: 目的に合わせたSQLクエリを自動生成
description_en: Generate optimized SQL queries for any use case
content: You are a senior database engineer. Write an optimized SQL query for the following task. Database type: [MySQL/PostgreSQL/SQLite/BigQuery]. Task description: [WHAT YOU WANT TO QUERY]. Table structure: [PASTE YOUR SCHEMA or DESCRIBE TABLES]. Requirements: [FILTERS/SORTING/GROUPING NEEDED]. Provide: 1) The complete SQL query with comments, 2) Explanation of each clause and why it's used, 3) Index recommendations for performance, 4) Alternative approach if applicable, 5) Common pitfalls to avoid with this query type. If the query is complex, break it down step by step before showing the final version.

title: README Generator
description_ja: プロジェクトのREADMEを自動生成
description_en: Generate a professional README for any project
content: Generate a comprehensive README.md for my project. Project name: [NAME]. What it does: [DESCRIPTION]. Tech stack: [TECHNOLOGIES]. Target users: [WHO USES IT]. Include these sections: 1) Project title + badges (build status, license, version), 2) One-line description + screenshot placeholder, 3) Key features (bullet list), 4) Prerequisites, 5) Installation (step-by-step commands), 6) Configuration (environment variables table), 7) Usage examples with code blocks, 8) API reference (if applicable), 9) Contributing guidelines, 10) License. Use clear markdown formatting, include emoji section headers for readability, and add a Quick Start section for users who want to skip the details.

title: X (Twitter) Thread Writer
description_ja: バズるXスレッドを自動生成
description_en: Write a viral X/Twitter thread
content: Write a high-engagement X/Twitter thread about [TOPIC] for [AUDIENCE]. Thread length: 8-12 tweets. Tweet 1: Hook - a bold claim, surprising stat, or provocative question that stops the scroll. Tweets 2-9: One idea per tweet, each ending with a reason to read the next. Vary sentence length. Use line breaks for readability. Include 1-2 tweets with a list format. Tweet 10+: Summary of key takeaways. Final tweet: CTA (follow, retweet, reply with their experience). Rules: No filler words. Every tweet must stand alone. Max 250 characters each. Add relevant emoji sparingly.

title: LinkedIn Thought Leadership Post
description_ja: LinkedInで専門性を示す投稿を作成
description_en: Write a LinkedIn post that builds authority
content: Write a LinkedIn thought leadership post for [NAME/BRAND] in [INDUSTRY]. Topic: [TOPIC]. Goal: [GOAL - e.g., attract clients/build network/share insight]. Format: Opening hook (1-2 lines, no 'I am excited to share'), Personal story or observation (3-4 lines), Key insight or lesson (3-5 bullet points), Broader implication for the industry, Closing question to drive comments. Tone: Professional but human. No jargon. No buzzwords like 'synergy' or 'leveraging'. Length: 150-300 words. End with 3 relevant hashtag suggestions.

title: YouTube Community Post Pack
description_ja: YouTube コミュニティ投稿を一括作成
description_en: Create a week of YouTube community posts
content: Create 7 YouTube Community tab posts for [CHANNEL NAME] in [NICHE]. Mix these formats: 2 polls (engaging questions relevant to your audience), 2 behind-the-scenes text updates, 1 teaser for upcoming video, 1 resource share (tool/book/tip), 1 personal story or opinion. Each post should: Be under 300 characters, Feel conversational, Drive engagement (question, poll, or reaction request), Reinforce channel identity. Also suggest the best days/times to post each type for maximum visibility.

title: Instagram Stories Sequence
description_ja: インスタのストーリーズシナリオを設計
description_en: Design an engaging Instagram Stories sequence
content: Design a 7-slide Instagram Stories sequence for [BRAND/PERSON] about [TOPIC]. For each slide specify: Slide purpose, Visual direction (background color/image type), Text overlay (exact copy, max 10 words), Interactive element if any (poll/quiz/question sticker/slider), Transition suggestion. Sequence arc: Slide 1 - Hook/attention grabber, Slides 2-5 - Value delivery, Slide 6 - Social proof or credibility, Slide 7 - CTA with link sticker copy. Keep each slide readable in 3 seconds. Total story should feel like a mini-journey, not a sales pitch.

title: Collaboration DM Outreach
description_ja: コラボ依頼DMのテンプレートを作成
description_en: Write DMs that get collaboration replies
content: Write 3 collaboration outreach DM templates for [YOUR BRAND/NAME] reaching out to [TARGET - e.g., influencer/brand/creator] in [NICHE]. Context: Your account size: [SIZE], What you're offering: [OFFER], What you want: [GOAL]. For each template: Keep it under 150 words, Open with a genuine specific compliment (not generic), State the value proposition clearly, Make the ask easy to say yes to, Include a soft close. Template 1: Paid collaboration pitch. Template 2: Barter/exchange pitch. Template 3: Follow-up message if no reply after 5 days. Tone: Warm, professional, peer-to-peer (not fan-to-idol).

title: Social Media Bio Optimizer
description_ja: 各SNSのプロフィール文を最適化
description_en: Optimize your bio for every platform
content: Optimize the social media bio for [NAME/BRAND] across all platforms. About them: [WHAT THEY DO], [WHO THEY HELP], [KEY RESULT THEY DELIVER], [UNIQUE ANGLE]. Write optimized bios for: Instagram (150 chars - line-break format with emoji), X/Twitter (160 chars - punchy, personality-forward), LinkedIn (220 chars - professional, keyword-rich), TikTok (80 chars - fun, hook-driven), Pinterest (160 chars - SEO-optimized). For each bio: state the target keyword, explain the strategic choice, and provide 1 alternative version. Also suggest a profile photo direction and pinned post/highlight strategy.

title: Crisis Response Post
description_ja: 炎上・批判へのSNS対応文を作成
description_en: Write a professional crisis response for social media
content: Write a social media crisis response for [BRAND/PERSON] regarding [SITUATION/CRITICISM]. Tone required: [TONE - e.g., apologetic/clarifying/firm]. Provide: 1) Initial holding statement (post within 1 hour - acknowledge, no details yet), 2) Full response post (24-48 hours - address facts, show accountability, state next steps), 3) Follow-up update post (after resolution), 4) Direct reply templates for angry comments (3 variations: empathetic/factual/de-escalating), 5) What NOT to say (5 phrases to avoid). Principles: Never be defensive. Never delete legitimate criticism. Always humanize. Keep it concise.

title: Viral Hook Formula Pack
description_ja: バズるフック文を大量生成
description_en: Generate 20 viral hooks for any topic
content: Generate 20 viral social media hooks for content about [TOPIC] targeting [AUDIENCE]. Create 4 hooks for each of these proven formulas: 1) Contrarian (Everyone thinks X, but actually Y), 2) Curiosity gap (The reason why [unexpected thing]), 3) Social proof (How [relatable person] achieved [result]), 4) Numbered list (X things [audience] doesn't know about [topic]), 5) Personal story opener (I [did something], here's what happened). For each hook: write the hook, explain why it works psychologically, and rate its viral potential (high/medium) with reasoning. Hooks should be platform-agnostic (work for IG, TikTok, X, LinkedIn).

title: Comment Strategy Builder
description_ja: エンゲージメントを上げるコメント戦略
description_en: Build a comment engagement strategy
content: Build a comment engagement strategy for [BRAND/CREATOR] in [NICHE] to grow visibility and followers organically. Provide: 1) Target accounts to comment on (5 types with rationale), 2) Comment frameworks (5 templates that add value, not just emoji), 3) Timing strategy (when to comment for maximum visibility), 4) Reply strategy for own post comments (how to respond to drive more replies), 5) Community-building comment tactics (how to create sub-conversations), 6) What to avoid (5 comment types that look spammy), 7) Weekly comment routine (daily 15-minute action plan). Goal: Position [BRAND] as a valuable community member, not a self-promoter.

title: Pinterest SEO Description Pack
description_ja: Pinterest用SEO最適化の説明文を作成
description_en: Write SEO-optimized Pinterest pin descriptions
content: Write 10 SEO-optimized Pinterest pin descriptions for [NICHE/TOPIC] targeting [AUDIENCE]. Primary keyword: [KEYWORD]. For each description: Length 100-200 characters, Start with the primary keyword naturally, Include 2-3 secondary keywords, Describe what the pin delivers (not what it looks like), End with a soft CTA, Include 5 relevant hashtags. Also provide: A board title and description for this content, 10 keyword variations to use across pins, Tips for pin image optimization (dimensions, text overlay, color psychology for this niche). Goal: maximize saves, clicks, and long-term organic reach.

title: SEO Content Strategy
description_ja: SEOコンテンツ戦略を設計
description_en: Build a 3-month SEO content strategy
content: Create a 3-month SEO content strategy for [WEBSITE/BRAND] in [INDUSTRY]. Target audience: [AUDIENCE]. Main goal: [GOAL - traffic/leads/sales]. Provide: 1) Keyword research framework (seed keywords, long-tail variations, search intent classification), 2) Content pillars (3-5 main topics that own a niche), 3) Monthly content calendar (4 articles/month with title, target keyword, search volume estimate, content type), 4) Internal linking strategy, 5) Content upgrade ideas for each article (lead magnets), 6) Metrics to track monthly (rankings, traffic, conversions), 7) Quick-win opportunities (low competition, high intent keywords to target first). Prioritize buyer-intent keywords.

title: Referral Program Designer
description_ja: 紹介プログラムの設計と文言作成
description_en: Design a customer referral program
content: Design a complete customer referral program for [BUSINESS] selling [PRODUCT/SERVICE] at [PRICE POINT]. Current customer base: [SIZE]. Create: 1) Program structure (what referrer gets, what new customer gets, timing), 2) Program name and tagline, 3) Email to existing customers announcing the program, 4) Reminder email sequence (3 emails over 30 days), 5) Social share copy templates (3 versions customers can use), 6) Landing page copy for the referral page, 7) Thank you message when referral converts, 8) Program rules and edge cases to consider, 9) Tech stack recommendation (tools to manage it), 10) KPIs to measure success. Make the incentive feel generous, not transactional.

title: Google Ads Copy Generator
description_ja: Google広告のコピーを複数パターン生成
description_en: Write high-converting Google Ads copy
content: Write Google Ads copy for [PRODUCT/SERVICE]. Target keyword: [KEYWORD]. Target audience: [AUDIENCE]. Landing page URL: [URL]. USP: [UNIQUE SELLING POINT]. Create: 3 Responsive Search Ad variations, each with: 15 headlines (30 chars max each - mix of keyword-rich, benefit-focused, CTA-driven), 4 descriptions (90 chars max each - expand on benefit, include CTA). Also provide: 5 callout extensions, 3 sitelink extensions with descriptions, 2 structured snippet headers with values. Flag which headlines to pin to position 1/2/3. Note: avoid superlatives like 'best' without proof. Focus on specificity over hype.

title: Webinar Funnel Copy
description_ja: ウェビナー集客からフォローアップまでの文言
description_en: Write a complete webinar funnel copy
content: Write complete copy for a webinar funnel for [TOPIC] hosted by [NAME/BRAND]. Audience: [TARGET]. Outcome promise: [WHAT THEY'LL LEARN/GET]. Create: 1) Registration page headline + subheadline (3 variations), 2) Bullet points of what attendees will learn (5 items), 3) Host bio section, 4) Registration confirmation email, 5) Reminder emails (3: 1 week before, 1 day before, 1 hour before), 6) Show-up incentive idea, 7) During-webinar engagement questions (5), 8) Pitch transition script (2-minute bridge from content to offer), 9) Replay email for no-shows, 10) Post-webinar follow-up sequence (3 emails). Tone: educational and genuine, not hyped.

title: Influencer Brief Creator
description_ja: インフルエンサーへのブリーフを作成
description_en: Create a professional influencer campaign brief
content: Create a professional influencer campaign brief for [BRAND] partnering with [INFLUENCER TYPE] to promote [PRODUCT/SERVICE]. Campaign goal: [GOAL]. Budget range: [BUDGET]. Create a complete brief including: 1) Campaign overview (objectives, target audience, key message), 2) Deliverables (exact content pieces, formats, quantities, deadlines), 3) Key messaging points (must-include and must-avoid), 4) Brand guidelines summary (tone, visual dos and don'ts), 5) Hashtags and tags required, 6) Disclosure requirements (#ad/#sponsored placement), 7) Approval process and timeline, 8) Performance metrics and reporting requirements, 9) Payment terms, 10) Content usage rights. Keep it clear enough that a first-time brand partner can execute without confusion.

title: Abandoned Cart Email Sequence
description_ja: カゴ落ちメールのシーケンスを作成
description_en: Write an abandoned cart recovery email sequence
content: Write a 3-email abandoned cart recovery sequence for [STORE/BRAND] selling [PRODUCT] at [PRICE]. Customer left: [WHAT THEY LEFT IN CART]. For each email provide subject line (+ 2 alternatives), preview text, full email body, and CTA button text. Email 1 (1 hour after abandonment): Gentle reminder, no pressure, highlight product benefit. Email 2 (24 hours): Address common objections, add social proof (review snippet), restate value. Email 3 (72 hours): Create urgency (limited stock/time), offer incentive if appropriate, last chance tone. Rules: Never be pushy. Personalize with product name. Mobile-optimized copy (short paragraphs). Include a PS line in each email.

title: Competitor Analysis Framework
description_ja: 競合分析レポートを自動作成
description_en: Create a thorough competitor analysis
content: Create a competitor analysis framework for [YOUR BUSINESS] vs. competitors in [INDUSTRY]. My business: [BRIEF DESCRIPTION]. Analyze these dimensions for each competitor: 1) Positioning & messaging (headline, tagline, core promise), 2) Product/service offering (features, pricing tiers, packaging), 3) Content & SEO strategy (topics, traffic estimate, keyword gaps), 4) Social media presence (platforms, posting frequency, engagement rate, content style), 5) Customer reviews (recurring praise and complaints), 6) Funnel & conversion tactics (lead magnets, email sequences, retargeting). Then provide: A positioning gap analysis (where you can win), 3 strategic opportunities based on competitor weaknesses, 2 threats to address. Competitors to analyze: [LIST COMPETITORS].

title: Testimonial & Review Request
description_ja: 口コミ・レビュー依頼メールを作成
description_en: Write review request emails that actually work
content: Write testimonial and review request sequences for [BUSINESS] after a customer completes [PURCHASE/SERVICE]. Create: 1) Post-purchase email (3 days after): check-in, soft ask for feedback, 2) Review request email (7 days after): direct ask for review with specific platforms (Google/Amazon/Trustpilot), 3) Testimonial request email (14 days after): ask for detailed story-format testimonial with guiding questions, 4) Follow-up for non-responders (21 days), 5) Thank you email when review is received. For the testimonial request, include 5 guiding questions that elicit specific, story-driven responses (not just 'it was great'). Also provide SMS versions (under 160 chars) for each touchpoint.

title: Pricing Page Copy
description_ja: 料金ページのコピーを最適化
description_en: Write pricing page copy that converts
content: Write pricing page copy for [PRODUCT/SERVICE] with [NUMBER] tiers. Tiers: [DESCRIBE EACH TIER AND PRICE]. Target customer: [AUDIENCE]. Most popular plan: [PLAN NAME]. Create: 1) Pricing section headline + subheadline, 2) Each plan name (if not set), tagline, and feature list copy (benefits-framed, not feature-framed), 3) 'Most popular' badge copy, 4) CTA button copy for each tier, 5) FAQ section (8 questions: pricing objections, cancellation, upgrades, payment methods), 6) Money-back guarantee copy, 7) Enterprise/custom plan CTA section, 8) Social proof strip (logos or stats), 9) Comparison table headers. Anchor the middle tier as the obvious choice without making the others seem bad.

title: Discount Campaign Copy
description_ja: セール・割引キャンペーンの文言作成
description_en: Write urgency-driven discount campaign copy
content: Write a complete discount campaign for [BUSINESS] offering [DISCOUNT AMOUNT/TYPE] on [PRODUCT/SERVICE]. Campaign dates: [START] to [END]. Reason for sale (if any): [REASON - e.g., anniversary/seasonal/clearance]. Create: 1) Campaign name and tagline, 2) Announcement email (day 1), 3) Mid-campaign reminder email (day 3-4), 4) Last chance email (final 24 hours), 5) Social media posts (5: announcement, value reminder, testimonial, FAQ, last chance), 6) SMS copy (3 messages: launch, midpoint, close), 7) Exit-intent popup copy, 8) Countdown timer copy variations. Rules: Make scarcity real (not fake). Lead with value first, discount second. Each touchpoint should feel fresh, not repetitive.

title: Typography System Designer
description_ja: タイポグラフィシステムを設計
description_en: Design a complete typography system
content: Design a complete typography system for [BRAND/PROJECT]. Brand personality: [ADJECTIVES]. Platform: [Web/App/Print/All]. Audience: [AUDIENCE]. Provide: 1) Font pairing recommendation (display + body + mono if needed) with rationale and Google Fonts/system alternatives, 2) Type scale (xs through 5xl with px values and rem equivalents), 3) Line height and letter spacing rules for each size, 4) Font weight usage guide (when to use each weight), 5) Heading hierarchy (H1-H6 with use cases), 6) Body text specifications (optimal line length, paragraph spacing), 7) Special cases (captions, labels, quotes, code), 8) CSS custom properties setup for the system, 9) Accessibility check (contrast ratios, minimum sizes), 10) Common mistakes to avoid.

title: Brand Guidelines Document
description_ja: ブランドガイドラインを作成
description_en: Create a complete brand guidelines document
content: Create a comprehensive brand guidelines document for [BRAND NAME]. Industry: [INDUSTRY]. Brand values: [VALUES]. Create an outline and copy for each section: 1) Brand story & mission (2-3 paragraphs), 2) Logo usage rules (clear space, minimum sizes, color variations, don'ts), 3) Color system (primary/secondary/neutral/semantic with hex, RGB, CMYK values and usage rules), 4) Typography system (fonts, hierarchy, usage), 5) Imagery style guide (photography direction, illustration style, what to avoid), 6) Tone of voice (3 key characteristics, do/don't examples for each), 7) UI component principles (spacing, border radius, shadow philosophy), 8) Brand don'ts (10 specific violations to prevent). Format as a scannable document.

title: Dark Mode Design Checklist
description_ja: ダークモード対応のデザインチェックリスト
description_en: Create a dark mode design system
content: Create a complete dark mode design system for [APP/WEBSITE]. Existing light mode colors: [LIST YOUR CURRENT COLORS OR DESCRIBE]. Provide: 1) Dark mode color mapping (for each light color, provide the dark equivalent with hex values and reasoning), 2) Surface hierarchy (background levels: base, elevated, overlay), 3) Text color system (primary/secondary/disabled/inverse for dark), 4) Shadow adjustments (shadows don't work in dark mode - provide glow/border alternatives), 5) Image and illustration handling guidelines, 6) Component-by-component dark mode specs (cards, buttons, inputs, modals, navigation), 7) CSS custom property structure for theme switching, 8) Common dark mode mistakes to avoid, 9) Testing checklist (15 items), 10) User preference detection code snippet.

title: App Icon Design Brief
description_ja: アプリアイコンのデザインブリーフ
description_en: Write an app icon design brief
content: Write a detailed app icon design brief for [APP NAME] in [CATEGORY]. App description: [WHAT IT DOES]. Target users: [AUDIENCE]. Brand personality: [ADJECTIVES]. Provide: 1) Icon concept directions (3 distinct concepts with rationale), 2) Visual metaphor options (literal/abstract/lettermark), 3) Color psychology recommendation for this category, 4) Shape language guide (rounded vs geometric vs organic), 5) Competitor icon analysis (what to differentiate from), 6) Size and grid specifications (iOS/Android requirements), 7) What to avoid (common pitfalls in this app category), 8) Variations needed (full color, monochrome, small size adaptations), 9) Testing criteria (does it work at 29px? In dark/light mode?), 10) Deliverable specifications.

title: Presentation Design System
description_ja: プレゼン用デザインシステムを構築
description_en: Build a presentation design system
content: Build a presentation design system for [BRAND/COMPANY] for use in [CONTEXT - e.g., investor pitches/client presentations/internal meetings]. Brand colors: [COLORS]. Create: 1) Slide layout library (10 essential layouts with descriptions: title, agenda, section divider, content, comparison, data visualization, quote, team, timeline, CTA), 2) Color usage rules per layout type, 3) Typography specs (title size, subtitle, body, caption for slides), 4) Data visualization style guide (chart colors, label styles, when to use each chart type), 5) Icon and image treatment guidelines, 6) Animation principles (what to animate and what not to), 7) Slide count recommendations by presentation type, 8) Common presentation design mistakes to avoid.

title: Illustration Style Guide
description_ja: イラストレーションのスタイルガイド
description_en: Create an illustration style guide
content: Create an illustration style guide for [BRAND]. Brand personality: [ADJECTIVES]. Use cases: [WHERE ILLUSTRATIONS WILL APPEAR - e.g., website/app/marketing]. Define: 1) Style direction (flat/outline/3D/painterly/geometric - with rationale), 2) Character design principles (if applicable: proportions, expressions, diversity guidelines), 3) Color palette for illustrations (can differ from brand palette), 4) Line weight and stroke rules, 5) Perspective and composition principles, 6) Texture and grain usage, 7) What emotions/scenarios to depict, 8) What to avoid (cultural sensitivities, stereotypes, overused concepts), 9) Reference mood board description (5-7 adjectives and existing work to reference), 10) File format and delivery specifications. Include 3 sample illustration briefs as examples.

title: UX Copy & Microcopy Guide
description_ja: UXコピーとマイクロコピーの設計
description_en: Write UX copy and microcopy for your product
content: Write comprehensive UX copy and microcopy for [PRODUCT/APP]. Brand voice: [TONE]. Target user: [USER]. Create copy for: 1) Onboarding screens (welcome message, feature highlights, permission requests for camera/notifications/location), 2) Empty states (6 scenarios: no data, no results, error, first-time, filtered empty, offline), 3) Error messages (form validation, server errors, network errors - each with what went wrong + how to fix), 4) Loading states (skeleton screens copy, progress messages), 5) Confirmation dialogs (5 scenarios: delete/cancel/irreversible actions), 6) Success messages (5 scenarios), 7) Tooltips and helper text (5 UI elements), 8) Button copy alternatives (10 CTAs, 3 variations each). Principle: be human, be specific, never blame the user.

title: Design Critique Framework
description_ja: デザインフィードバックの構造化
description_en: Structure a thorough design critique
content: Conduct a structured design critique for [DESIGN TYPE - e.g., landing page/app screen/brand identity]. Paste or describe the design: [DESCRIPTION OR URL]. Evaluate across these dimensions: 1) Visual hierarchy (does the eye go where intended?), 2) Consistency (spacing, color, typography patterns), 3) Contrast and accessibility (WCAG AA compliance check), 4) Content clarity (is the message immediately understood?), 5) Interaction design (are interactive elements obvious?), 6) Emotional response (does it feel right for the brand?), 7) Mobile responsiveness considerations, 8) Loading performance implications. For each dimension: rate it (strong/needs work/critical issue), explain why, and give a specific actionable recommendation. End with the top 3 changes for maximum impact.

title: Social Media Template Kit
description_ja: SNS用テンプレートキットの設計仕様
description_en: Design a social media template kit spec
content: Design a social media template kit specification for [BRAND]. Platforms: [PLATFORMS]. Content types: [TYPES - e.g., tips/quotes/product/announcements]. Brand assets: [COLORS/FONTS]. Create specs for: 1) Template list (12 templates - name, platform, dimensions, content slots), 2) Grid system for each template (how to divide the canvas), 3) Text zone specifications (max characters per zone, font size, weight), 4) Image treatment rules (overlay opacity, blur, crop ratios), 5) Color combination rules (which brand colors pair for which content type), 6) Logo placement rules, 7) Hashtag/handle placement, 8) Carousel design continuity rules, 9) Animation guidelines if applicable, 10) File naming convention and folder structure. Also provide a 30-day content calendar showing which template to use each day.

title: Design System Audit
description_ja: デザインシステムの監査と改善提案
description_en: Audit and improve a design system
content: Conduct a design system audit for [PRODUCT/BRAND]. Current state: [BRIEF DESCRIPTION OF EXISTING SYSTEM]. Audit these areas: 1) Component inventory (list components that should exist vs what exists - identify gaps), 2) Consistency check (color token usage, spacing scale adherence, typography application), 3) Accessibility compliance (color contrast, touch targets, focus states), 4) Documentation quality (is each component documented with use cases and don'ts?), 5) Naming conventions (are names logical, scalable, platform-agnostic?), 6) Maintenance health (how often updated? Who owns it?), 7) Adoption rate (are teams actually using it?). Deliverable: Prioritized list of fixes (critical/important/nice-to-have), a 3-month improvement roadmap, and governance recommendations.

title: Anti-Aging Skincare Protocol
description_ja: エイジングケアのスキンケアプロトコル
description_en: Create an evidence-based anti-aging routine
content: You are a dermatologist specializing in cosmetic skincare. Create an evidence-based anti-aging skincare protocol for: Age: [AGE], Skin type: [TYPE], Main aging concerns: [CONCERNS - e.g., wrinkles/dark spots/sagging/dullness], Budget: [BUDGET], Current routine: [DESCRIBE OR 'none']. Provide: 1) Morning routine (step-by-step with timing), 2) Evening routine (step-by-step), 3) Weekly treatments, 4) Key ingredients to prioritize (retinoids, peptides, antioxidants, etc.) with scientific rationale, 5) Ingredients to avoid combining, 6) Lifestyle factors (sleep, diet, sun protection), 7) Professional treatments to consider, 8) Realistic timeline for results, 9) Red flags that warrant a dermatologist visit, 10) Budget breakdown (splurge vs save recommendations).

title: Men's Grooming Routine
description_ja: メンズグルーミングの完全ルーティン
description_en: Build a complete men's grooming routine
content: Build a complete grooming routine for men. Profile: Age: [AGE], Skin type: [TYPE], Hair type: [TYPE], Beard: [YES/NO - if yes, length/style], Lifestyle: [LIFESTYLE - e.g., office/active/outdoors], Time available daily: [MINUTES], Budget: [BUDGET]. Create: 1) Daily face routine (morning + evening, max 5 steps each), 2) Hair care routine (wash frequency, styling guide), 3) Beard care routine if applicable, 4) Weekly treatments (exfoliation, deep conditioning), 5) Product recommendations by category with affordable and premium options, 6) Key ingredients to look for in men's products, 7) Common grooming mistakes to avoid, 8) Travel kit essentials, 9) Seasonal adjustments, 10) When to see a dermatologist vs. handle at home.

title: Body Care Ritual Designer
description_ja: ボディケアルーティンを設計
description_en: Design a luxurious body care ritual
content: Design a complete body care ritual for [NAME/PROFILE]. Skin concerns: [CONCERNS - e.g., dryness/stretch marks/keratosis pilaris/uneven tone], Lifestyle: [ACTIVE/SEDENTARY/MIXED], Preferred texture: [LIGHTWEIGHT/RICH/NO PREFERENCE], Scent preference: [PREFERENCE], Budget: [BUDGET]. Create: 1) Shower routine (pre-shower dry brushing? water temperature? cleansing order), 2) Post-shower ritual (timing is critical - explain the 3-minute window), 3) Body moisturizing routine (technique + product types for different body zones), 4) Weekly exfoliation protocol (physical vs chemical for body), 5) Hand and foot care routine, 6) Problem area treatments (stretch marks, rough elbows, dark underarms), 7) Summer vs winter adjustments, 8) DIY treatment recipes (2-3 simple options), 9) Product layering order, 10) Ingredient glossary for body care.

title: Pre-Event Beauty Timeline
description_ja: イベント前の美容タイムラインを設計
description_en: Plan a beauty prep timeline for any event
content: Create a detailed beauty preparation timeline for [EVENT TYPE - e.g., wedding/job interview/photo shoot/gala] happening in [TIME FROM NOW]. Current skin/hair condition: [DESCRIBE]. Goal look: [DESCRIBE]. Create a reverse timeline: 4 weeks before (treatments to start, habits to build), 3 weeks before (adjustments, trial run recommendations), 2 weeks before (what to avoid: new products, harsh treatments), 1 week before (prep treatments, hair mask schedule), 3 days before (specific treatments, product patch tests), Day before (full routine to follow, what to avoid), Day of (morning routine, timing for makeup/hair, touch-up kit list). Include: what NOT to try for the first time, emergency fixes for common issues (breakout/puffiness/bad hair day), and a packing checklist for touch-ups.

title: Clean Beauty Ingredient Decoder
description_ja: クリーンビューティーの成分解析
description_en: Decode beauty ingredients for clean beauty
content: Act as a clean beauty formulation expert. Analyze the following product ingredient list and provide a consumer-friendly report. Product: [PRODUCT NAME]. Ingredients: [PASTE INGREDIENT LIST]. Provide: 1) Safety rating overview (clean/moderate concerns/avoid), 2) Top 5 ingredients explained (what they do, why they're in the formula), 3) Any potentially concerning ingredients (with scientific context, not fearmongering), 4) Ingredients to watch for your specific concern: [CONCERN - e.g., acne/sensitivity/hormonal], 5) Missing key ingredients for your goals, 6) How this compares to clean beauty standards (EWG, EU regulations), 7) Recommended alternatives if needed, 8) Shelf life and preservation assessment, 9) Sustainability notes (packaging, sourcing), 10) Verdict: worth the price?

title: Nail Care & Art Guide
description_ja: ネイルケアとネイルアートのガイド
description_en: Create a nail care and nail art guide
content: Create a comprehensive nail care and nail art guide for [EXPERIENCE LEVEL - beginner/intermediate/advanced]. Nail condition: [DESCRIBE - weak/brittle/ridged/healthy]. Goals: [GOALS - growth/art/maintenance]. Provide: 1) Nail health assessment and strengthening routine, 2) Essential nail care tools (must-have vs nice-to-have), 3) Basic manicure step-by-step (prep, shape, base coat, polish, top coat), 4) 3 nail art designs matched to skill level with step-by-step instructions, 5) Color palette suggestions for [SEASON] and current trends, 6) Gel vs regular polish comparison for this person's lifestyle, 7) How to make manicure last 2+ weeks, 8) DIY nail treatments (strengthening soak, cuticle oil recipe), 9) Common nail mistakes and fixes, 10) When nail symptoms indicate health issues.

title: Fragrance Profile Builder
description_ja: 自分に合った香水プロフィールを作成
description_en: Find your perfect fragrance profile
content: Act as a master perfumer and fragrance consultant. Build a personalized fragrance profile for: Personality: [DESCRIBE], Lifestyle: [LIFESTYLE], Scents I currently like: [LIST], Scents I dislike: [LIST], Occasions to wear: [OCCASIONS], Season: [SEASON], Budget: [BUDGET]. Provide: 1) Fragrance family recommendation (floral/woody/oriental/fresh/fougère) with explanation, 2) Top/middle/base note preferences analysis, 3) 5 specific fragrance recommendations (2 drugstore/2 mid-range/1 luxury) with where to smell them, 4) How to test fragrances properly (the 30-minute rule), 5) Layering techniques for this profile, 6) Application points for longevity, 7) Seasonal wardrobe approach, 8) How to store fragrances, 9) Fragrance vocabulary guide (projection, sillage, longevity), 10) Niche fragrance houses to explore for this profile.

title: Supplement & Wellness Beauty Stack
description_ja: 美容目的のサプリメントスタックを設計
description_en: Design a beauty supplement stack
content: Design an evidence-based beauty supplement stack for: Goals: [GOALS - hair growth/skin glow/nail strength/anti-aging], Age: [AGE], Dietary restrictions: [RESTRICTIONS], Current supplements: [LIST], Budget: [MONTHLY BUDGET]. Provide: 1) Core supplements (tier 1 - highest evidence: collagen, biotin, etc.), 2) Supporting supplements (tier 2 - good evidence for specific goals), 3) Lifestyle supplements (tier 3 - optimize overall health for beauty), 4) For each supplement: dosage, timing, food pairings, quality markers to look for in a brand, 5) Supplement synergies (what works better together), 6) Interactions to avoid (with medications or other supplements), 7) Realistic timeline for results, 8) Food-first alternatives for each supplement, 9) How to track results, 10) Red flags - when to stop and consult a doctor. Note: cite mechanism of action for each recommendation.

title: Seasonal Skincare Transition Guide
description_ja: 季節の変わり目のスキンケア移行ガイド
description_en: Transition your skincare routine between seasons
content: Create a seasonal skincare transition guide from [CURRENT SEASON] to [UPCOMING SEASON] for: Skin type: [TYPE], Location climate: [HUMID/DRY/MODERATE], Current routine: [DESCRIBE BRIEFLY], Main concerns: [CONCERNS]. Provide: 1) Why skin changes between these seasons (science behind it), 2) Products to swap out (and why), 3) Products to add (and why), 4) Texture adjustments (lightweight → richer or vice versa), 5) Ingredient focus shifts (e.g., hyaluronic acid in winter → niacinamide in summer), 6) SPF strategy adjustment, 7) Weekly treatment adjustments, 8) Diet and hydration tips for this transition, 9) Common skin reactions during this transition and how to handle them, 10) 2-week transition plan (gradual introduction of new products to avoid overloading skin).

title: Eyebrow Shaping & Styling Guide
description_ja: 顔の形に合わせた眉毛デザインガイド
description_en: Find your perfect eyebrow shape
content: Create a personalized eyebrow shaping and styling guide for: Face shape: [OVAL/ROUND/SQUARE/HEART/OBLONG], Current brow situation: [DESCRIBE - sparse/thick/uneven/over-plucked], Hair color: [COLOR], Makeup style: [NATURAL/GLAM/MINIMAL], Skill level: [BEGINNER/INTERMEDIATE], Preferred brow look: [NATURAL/DEFINED/DRAMATIC]. Provide: 1) Ideal brow shape for this face shape (with the 3-point mapping technique), 2) Arch placement and height recommendation, 3) Recommended thickness and tail length, 4) Brow product recommendations (pencil/pomade/gel/powder) for hair color and desired look, 5) Step-by-step shaping technique, 6) How to handle sparse areas, 7) Maintenance schedule (waxing/threading/tweezing frequency), 8) Common brow mistakes for this face shape, 9) Bold brow vs. natural brow tutorial, 10) Brow lamination and tinting - worth it for this profile?

title: Podcast Episode Script
description_ja: ポッドキャストのエピソード台本を作成
description_en: Write a complete podcast episode script
content: Write a complete podcast episode script for [PODCAST NAME] in [NICHE]. Episode topic: [TOPIC]. Target listener: [AUDIENCE]. Episode length: [LENGTH - e.g., 20/45/60 min]. Format: [SOLO/INTERVIEW/CO-HOST]. Create: 1) Intro hook (60 seconds - start with a story or bold statement, not 'welcome back'), 2) Episode overview teaser (what listeners will get), 3) Main content outline (3-5 segments with talking points and transitions), 4) Personal story or example to include, 5) Data or research points to reference, 6) Listener action step, 7) Outro (recap + CTA: subscribe/review/share), 8) Ad break placement suggestions (if applicable), 9) Show notes outline (summary, timestamps, links, resources mentioned), 10) Social media teaser clip script (60 seconds of the most shareable moment).

title: YouTube Video Script
description_ja: YouTubeの動画台本を作成
description_en: Write a high-retention YouTube video script
content: Write a high-retention YouTube video script for [CHANNEL] about [TOPIC]. Target viewer: [AUDIENCE]. Video length: [LENGTH]. Goal: [VIEWS/SUBSCRIBERS/SALES/EDUCATION]. Create: 1) Hook (first 30 seconds - open with the payoff, not the intro), 2) Pattern interrupt at 30-second mark to prevent drop-off, 3) Value loop setup ('by the end of this video you'll know...'), 4) Main content (3-5 sections with b-roll suggestions and on-screen text ideas), 5) Re-engagement hooks every 2-3 minutes ('but here's where it gets interesting...'), 6) Call-to-action placement (subscribe reminder at the right moment), 7) Outro (strong close, next video suggestion), 8) Chapter timestamps, 9) Title options (5 variations optimized for CTR), 10) Thumbnail concept description.

title: Case Study Writer
description_ja: 成果を伝えるケーススタディを作成
description_en: Write a compelling customer case study
content: Write a compelling customer case study for [YOUR BUSINESS] featuring [CLIENT NAME/TYPE]. Results achieved: [KEY RESULTS]. Create a complete case study with: 1) Headline (result-focused, e.g., 'How [Client] achieved [Result] in [Timeframe]'), 2) Client snapshot (industry, size, context - 2-3 lines), 3) The challenge (what problem they faced before you - 1-2 paragraphs with emotion), 4) Why they chose you (over alternatives - builds trust), 5) The solution (what you did, how you did it - specific and visual), 6) The results (quantified outcomes, before/after, timeline), 7) Client quote (3 quote options of varying length), 8) Key takeaways (3 bullets for skimmers), 9) CTA ('Ready for similar results?'). Also provide a 300-word short version for blog/email and a 3-tweet social version.

title: Press Release Generator
description_ja: プレスリリースを自動生成
description_en: Write a professional press release
content: Write a professional press release for [COMPANY/BRAND] announcing [NEWS - e.g., product launch/funding/partnership/award]. Company: [BRIEF DESCRIPTION]. News details: [DETAILS]. Date: [DATE]. Location: [CITY]. Create: 1) Headline (active voice, present tense, newsworthy angle), 2) Subheadline (supporting detail), 3) Dateline and lead paragraph (who/what/when/where/why in 2-3 sentences), 4) Body paragraphs (2-3: expand the story, add context, industry significance), 5) Executive quote (authentic-sounding, not corporate), 6) Company boilerplate (3-4 sentences, standard format), 7) Media contact information template, 8) 3 social media post variations to announce the same news, 9) Email subject line for journalist outreach, 10) Follow-up pitch email template (for if journalist doesn't respond).

title: Product Description Pack
description_ja: 商品説明文を複数形式で一括生成
description_en: Write product descriptions for every channel
content: Write product descriptions for [PRODUCT NAME] across all sales channels. Product details: [FEATURES], [MATERIALS/INGREDIENTS], [SIZE/DIMENSIONS], [BENEFITS], [TARGET USER]. Create: 1) Amazon/e-commerce long description (bullet points, keyword-rich, 300 words), 2) Short description for category pages (50 words, benefit-led), 3) Instagram caption version (conversational, emoji-friendly), 4) Email feature section (HTML-ready copy), 5) Unboxing experience description (for packaging inserts), 6) Comparison table copy (vs. competitor or vs. old version), 7) FAQ section (5 common questions + answers), 8) Gift description (for gift wrapping service or registry), 9) Technical specifications formatted cleanly, 10) Meta description for SEO (155 chars). Tone: [TONE]. Keywords to include: [KEYWORDS].

title: Speech & Presentation Script
description_ja: スピーチ・プレゼン台本を作成
description_en: Write a memorable speech or presentation script
content: Write a [LENGTH]-minute speech/presentation script for [SPEAKER] on the topic of [TOPIC] for [AUDIENCE] at [EVENT TYPE]. Speaker's key message: [MESSAGE]. Speaker's personal story to include: [STORY]. Create: 1) Opening hook (story, question, or surprising fact - not 'good morning everyone'), 2) Thesis/core message statement, 3) Main points (3 key arguments with supporting evidence), 4) Emotional story beat (makes the message memorable), 5) Data or research reference, 6) Audience interaction moment (question or show of hands), 7) Call to action (what you want them to do/think/feel), 8) Memorable closing line, 9) Speaker notes for delivery (pace, pause, emphasis marks), 10) Q&A preparation (5 likely questions + ideal answers).

title: About Page Copywriter
description_ja: AboutページのコピーをSEO対応で作成
description_en: Write an About page that builds trust
content: Write a compelling About page for [BRAND/PERSON]. Background: [YOUR STORY], Mission: [MISSION], Values: [VALUES], Who you serve: [AUDIENCE], What makes you different: [DIFFERENTIATOR], Credibility: [CREDENTIALS/EXPERIENCE]. Create: 1) Opening hook (start with them, not 'I was born...'), 2) The origin story (why you started this - include the messy moment), 3) What you believe (values in action, not abstract), 4) Who you serve (make them feel seen), 5) What you do and how (clear, jargon-free), 6) Credibility section (without bragging), 7) Personal humanity (1 surprising fact that makes you likable), 8) CTA (what's the next step?). Write 2 versions: long (600 words for website) and short (200 words for bio/press). SEO keyword to include: [KEYWORD].

title: Grant Proposal Outline
description_ja: 助成金申請書のアウトラインを作成
description_en: Write a grant proposal outline
content: Create a comprehensive grant proposal outline for [ORGANIZATION] applying for [GRANT NAME/TYPE] to fund [PROJECT]. Grant amount sought: [AMOUNT]. Funder priorities: [DESCRIBE]. Create: 1) Executive summary (1 page: problem, solution, impact, ask), 2) Organization background (mission, history, credibility, past successes), 3) Problem statement (data-driven need assessment), 4) Project description (goals, activities, timeline, methodology), 5) Evaluation plan (how you'll measure success - SMART metrics), 6) Sustainability plan (how the project continues after grant period), 7) Budget narrative (line items with justification), 8) Team qualifications section, 9) Letters of support template, 10) Common grant writing mistakes to avoid for this funder type. Also provide: 5 powerful opening sentences and a checklist of what to include before submitting.

title: Thread & Carousel Script Pack
description_ja: カルーセル投稿の台本を作成
description_en: Write carousel and thread scripts for any topic
content: Write a carousel/thread script for [TOPIC] targeting [AUDIENCE] on [PLATFORM - Instagram carousel/LinkedIn/X thread]. Goal: [EDUCATE/INSPIRE/CONVERT]. Create a 10-slide/tweet structure: Slide 1: Hook - Bold claim or counterintuitive statement (must stop the scroll), Slides 2-8: One insight per slide. Each must: be self-contained, use simple language, include a specific example or data point, end with a micro-cliffhanger or transition, Slide 9: Summary or framework (the thing they'll screenshot), Slide 10: CTA + your credibility. Rules: No slide needs context from a previous slide to make sense. Use numbers and specifics. Write at 7th grade reading level. Provide alternate hooks (3 options for slide 1) and 2 CTA variations.

title: Email Newsletter Issue Writer
description_ja: メールニュースレターの1号を丸ごと作成
description_en: Write a complete email newsletter issue
content: Write a complete email newsletter issue for [NEWSLETTER NAME] targeting [AUDIENCE]. This issue's theme: [THEME]. Newsletter format: [FORMAT - curated links/original essay/mixed]. Create: 1) Subject line (5 options: curiosity/benefit/personal/timely/question), 2) Preview text (pairs with each subject line), 3) Opening (personal, conversational, 2-3 sentences - what's top of mind), 4) Main feature section (400-600 words: original take on [THEME] with concrete advice), 5) Quick hits section (3-5 short items: links, tools, quotes with 1-sentence context), 6) Reader spotlight or Q&A (template), 7) Personal note/closing (build relationship, tease next issue), 8) PS line (highest-read part of any email - make it count), 9) Unsubscribe copy (make it human, not legal), 10) Plain text fallback version.

title: OKR Framework Builder
description_ja: OKRフレームワークを設計
description_en: Build a quarterly OKR framework
content: Build a quarterly OKR (Objectives and Key Results) framework for [COMPANY/TEAM] in [INDUSTRY]. Company stage: [STAGE - startup/growth/enterprise]. Quarter: [Q1/Q2/Q3/Q4]. Company priority this quarter: [PRIORITY]. Create: 1) 3 company-level Objectives (aspirational, qualitative, time-bound), 2) 3-5 Key Results per Objective (measurable, specific, challenging but achievable), 3) Team-level OKR breakdown (how to cascade to 3 departments), 4) OKR scoring guide (how to rate 0.0-1.0), 5) Weekly check-in template, 6) Mid-quarter review questions, 7) End-of-quarter retrospective format, 8) Common OKR mistakes to avoid, 9) How to handle OKRs when priorities shift mid-quarter, 10) OKR vs. KPI explanation for team buy-in.

title: Job Description Writer
description_ja: 採用力の高い求人票を作成
description_en: Write a job description that attracts top talent
content: Write a compelling job description for [JOB TITLE] at [COMPANY]. Company type: [STAGE/INDUSTRY]. Team context: [WHO THEY'LL WORK WITH]. Compensation range: [RANGE]. Create: 1) Opening hook (why this role matters, not boilerplate company description), 2) What you'll do (5-7 bullet points: outcome-focused, not task-lists), 3) What you'll own (key responsibilities framed as ownership), 4) What we're looking for (must-have skills separated from nice-to-have), 5) What we offer (beyond salary: growth, culture, flexibility), 6) Company culture paragraph (authentic, specific - avoid 'fast-paced environment'), 7) Interview process transparency section, 8) Inclusion statement (genuine, not copy-pasted), 9) Application instructions. Remove any language that could discourage underrepresented candidates. Test: Would you apply to this?

title: Client Proposal Template
description_ja: 受注率を上げる提案書を作成
description_en: Write a client proposal that wins projects
content: Write a client proposal for [YOUR SERVICE] for [CLIENT NAME/TYPE]. Project scope: [SCOPE]. Client's stated goal: [GOAL]. Client's underlying need: [DEEPER NEED]. Budget discussed: [BUDGET]. Create: 1) Executive summary (1 page: their problem, your solution, expected outcome), 2) Understanding section (prove you understand their situation - this wins the proposal), 3) Proposed approach (your methodology, why it works for them specifically), 4) Scope of work (clear deliverables, what's included AND what's not), 5) Timeline (phased with milestones), 6) Investment section (pricing framed as investment with ROI angle), 7) About us / credibility (brief, relevant proof), 8) Next steps (frictionless path to yes), 9) Terms summary (plain language), 10) FAQ section (3 likely objections answered proactively).

title: SWOT & Strategic Analysis
description_ja: SWOT分析と戦略的提言を作成
description_en: Create a strategic SWOT analysis with action plan
content: Conduct a comprehensive SWOT analysis and strategic action plan for [BUSINESS/PROJECT]. Business description: [DESCRIBE]. Industry context: [INDUSTRY SITUATION]. Main competitors: [COMPETITORS]. Current goal: [GOAL]. Create: 1) SWOT matrix (5 items per quadrant, specific and honest), 2) SO Strategies (use Strengths to exploit Opportunities - 3 strategies), 3) WO Strategies (overcome Weaknesses by pursuing Opportunities - 3 strategies), 4) ST Strategies (use Strengths to avoid Threats - 3 strategies), 5) WT Strategies (minimize Weaknesses and avoid Threats - 3 strategies), 6) Priority ranking of all 12 strategies, 7) 90-day action plan for top 3 strategies, 8) Key metrics to track for each strategy, 9) Resource requirements, 10) Decision criteria for when to pivot.

title: Pricing Strategy Advisor
description_ja: 価格戦略を設計・最適化
description_en: Design an optimal pricing strategy
content: Design a comprehensive pricing strategy for [PRODUCT/SERVICE]. Business model: [MODEL]. Target market: [MARKET]. Current price (if any): [PRICE]. Main competitors' prices: [PRICES]. Cost to deliver: [COST]. Analyze and recommend: 1) Pricing model options (per-seat/usage/flat/tiered/freemium) with pros/cons for this business, 2) Recommended pricing model with rationale, 3) Price anchoring strategy (how to structure tiers), 4) Psychological pricing tactics appropriate for this market, 5) Value-based pricing calculation (how to quantify your value), 6) Competitive positioning (premium/parity/penetration), 7) Discount and promotion strategy, 8) Price increase strategy (how to raise prices without losing customers), 9) Packaging and bundling options, 10) Price testing methodology.

title: Meeting Agenda & Facilitation Guide
description_ja: 会議のアジェンダと進行ガイドを作成
description_en: Create a productive meeting agenda and guide
content: Create a complete meeting agenda and facilitation guide for [MEETING TYPE - e.g., team standup/client kickoff/board review/retrospective]. Duration: [LENGTH]. Attendees: [ROLES]. Goal: [WHAT MUST BE DECIDED/ACHIEVED]. Create: 1) Pre-meeting prep checklist (what to send and when), 2) Agenda with time allocations (every minute planned), 3) Opening ritual (2-minute icebreaker or focus exercise), 4) Decision framework (how to reach decisions efficiently), 5) Parking lot protocol (how to handle off-topic items), 6) Facilitation tips for this meeting type, 7) How to handle dominant speakers and quiet attendees, 8) Closing ritual (action items, owners, deadlines), 9) Meeting notes template, 10) Follow-up email template (send within 1 hour). Also: what to do if the meeting goes off track.

title: Freelance Contract Essentials
description_ja: フリーランス契約書の必須要素を整理
description_en: Draft freelance contract essential clauses
content: Draft essential contract clauses for a freelance [SERVICE TYPE] agreement between [YOUR NAME/COMPANY] and a client. Project type: [TYPE]. Payment structure: [STRUCTURE]. Create plain-language versions of: 1) Scope of work definition (and change order process), 2) Payment terms (schedule, method, late payment penalty), 3) Intellectual property transfer (when ownership transfers), 4) Revision policy (what's included, what's extra), 5) Kill fee clause (client cancels mid-project), 6) Confidentiality/NDA clause, 7) Non-solicitation clause (they can't poach your subcontractors), 8) Warranty and liability limitations, 9) Dispute resolution process, 10) Project termination conditions. For each clause: write the plain language version AND flag what a lawyer might want to strengthen. Note: this is a starting point, not legal advice.

title: Performance Review Framework
description_ja: パフォーマンスレビューの構造と文言
description_en: Write a fair and effective performance review
content: Create a performance review framework for [ROLE] at [COMPANY TYPE]. Review period: [TIMEFRAME]. Create: 1) Self-assessment template (10 questions that prompt reflection, not just ratings), 2) Manager review template (evaluation criteria with behavioral anchors for each rating level), 3) Goals review section (assessing past goals + setting new ones), 4) Competency matrix for this role (5 core competencies with level descriptions), 5) Strengths section framing guide (how to give genuine, specific praise), 6) Development areas framing (how to give constructive feedback without demotivating), 7) Conversation guide (how to run the actual review meeting), 8) Action plan template (post-review development plan), 9) Calibration guide (for managers rating across teams), 10) Sample review language for 5 performance scenarios.

title: Crisis Communication Plan
description_ja: 危機対応コミュニケーションプランを作成
description_en: Build a crisis communication plan
content: Build a crisis communication plan for [ORGANIZATION TYPE] in [INDUSTRY]. Potential crisis scenarios: [LIST 2-3 LIKELY SCENARIOS]. Stakeholders: [KEY GROUPS]. Create: 1) Crisis classification system (levels 1-3 with triggers), 2) Response team structure (who does what in first 24 hours), 3) First 60 minutes checklist, 4) Internal communication protocol (employees first), 5) External statement templates for each crisis type, 6) Social media response protocol, 7) Media inquiry handling guide, 8) Spokesperson training talking points, 9) 'Dark site' or holding page content, 10) Post-crisis reputation recovery plan. Also include: what to say vs. what not to say, how to handle social media pile-ons, and how to communicate when facts are still unknown. Test the plan with a tabletop exercise outline.

title: Partnership Pitch Email
description_ja: パートナーシップ提案メールを作成
description_en: Write partnership pitch emails that get replies
content: Write a partnership pitch email sequence for [YOUR COMPANY] reaching out to [TARGET COMPANY TYPE] about a potential partnership around [PARTNERSHIP IDEA]. Your company: [BRIEF DESCRIPTION]. What you bring to the partnership: [VALUE]. What you want from them: [ASK]. Create: 1) Initial outreach email (cold - under 200 words, specific, clear value prop, easy yes), 2) Follow-up email (5 days later, add new angle or info), 3) Final follow-up (10 days after, polite close), 4) Warm intro request email (if you have a mutual connection), 5) Response to 'send more info' (what to include in the deck summary). For each email: subject line options (3), opening line that proves you know them, value exchange framing, soft CTA. Avoid: generic flattery, lengthy company histories, immediate asks for a call.

title: Unit Test Generator
description_ja: ユニットテストを自動生成
description_en: Generate comprehensive unit tests for your code
content: Generate comprehensive unit tests for the following code. Language/Framework: [LANGUAGE + TESTING FRAMEWORK - e.g., JavaScript/Jest, Python/pytest, Go/testing]. Code to test: [PASTE YOUR CODE]. Create tests covering: 1) Happy path (expected inputs produce expected outputs), 2) Edge cases (empty inputs, null values, zero, max values), 3) Error cases (invalid inputs, network failures, permission errors), 4) Boundary conditions, 5) Async behavior (if applicable). For each test: use descriptive test names that explain what's being tested and why it should pass, follow AAA pattern (Arrange/Act/Assert), include setup and teardown where needed. Also provide: a coverage report estimate, any untestable code that needs refactoring, and mock/stub recommendations for external dependencies.

title: System Architecture Designer
description_ja: システムアーキテクチャを設計
description_en: Design a scalable system architecture
content: Design a scalable system architecture for [SYSTEM TYPE - e.g., e-commerce platform/real-time chat/data pipeline]. Scale requirements: [USERS/REQUESTS PER SECOND]. Key constraints: [CONSTRAINTS - budget/latency/consistency]. Tech preferences: [PREFERRED STACK or 'flexible']. Create: 1) High-level architecture diagram description (components and how they connect), 2) Technology stack recommendation with rationale for each choice, 3) Data model overview (key entities and relationships), 4) API design approach (REST/GraphQL/gRPC + versioning strategy), 5) Caching strategy (what to cache, where, for how long), 6) Database selection and sharding strategy, 7) Authentication and authorization approach, 8) Deployment architecture (containers/serverless/VMs + orchestration), 9) Monitoring and observability plan, 10) Disaster recovery and backup strategy. Include trade-offs for each major decision.

title: Code Refactoring Guide
description_ja: コードのリファクタリング計画を作成
description_en: Plan and execute a code refactoring
content: Create a code refactoring plan for the following code. Language: [LANGUAGE]. Context: [WHAT THIS CODE DOES]. Code: [PASTE CODE]. Analyze and provide: 1) Code smell inventory (identify all issues: long methods, duplicate code, magic numbers, poor naming, etc.), 2) Prioritized refactoring list (impact vs. effort matrix), 3) Refactored version with inline comments explaining each change, 4) Design patterns that could improve this code (with examples), 5) SOLID principles violations and fixes, 6) Performance improvements identified, 7) Test coverage additions needed before refactoring, 8) Step-by-step refactoring sequence (to avoid breaking changes), 9) Metrics to validate improvement (complexity score, lines of code, test coverage), 10) What NOT to refactor and why (avoid over-engineering).

title: Environment Setup Script
description_ja: 開発環境のセットアップスクリプトを生成
description_en: Generate a development environment setup script
content: Generate a complete development environment setup script for [PROJECT TYPE] on [OS - macOS/Ubuntu/Windows]. Tech stack: [STACK]. Team size: [SIZE]. Create: 1) Prerequisites checklist (what must be installed manually first), 2) Automated setup script (bash/PowerShell) that installs all dependencies, 3) Environment variables template (.env.example with comments for each variable), 4) Docker Compose file (if applicable) for local services (database, cache, etc.), 5) VS Code workspace settings and recommended extensions list, 6) Git hooks setup (pre-commit linting/formatting), 7) Database seeding script, 8) Common setup troubleshooting (5 most common issues + fixes), 9) Onboarding README section (estimated time to set up + steps), 10) Health check command to verify everything is working.

title: Security Audit Checklist
description_ja: セキュリティ監査チェックリストを作成
description_en: Run a security audit on your codebase
content: Conduct a security audit on the following code/system. Language: [LANGUAGE]. Framework: [FRAMEWORK]. Code or description: [PASTE CODE OR DESCRIBE SYSTEM]. Check for: 1) OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities (SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, etc.), 2) Authentication and session management flaws, 3) Sensitive data exposure (hardcoded secrets, unencrypted PII), 4) Dependency vulnerabilities (known CVEs in packages used), 5) Input validation and sanitization gaps, 6) Authorization issues (privilege escalation, IDOR), 7) API security issues (rate limiting, authentication, CORS), 8) Logging and monitoring gaps (logging too much or too little), 9) Cryptography misuse, 10) Security headers (for web apps). For each finding: severity (critical/high/medium/low), explanation, exploit scenario, and specific fix with code example.

title: CI/CD Pipeline Designer
description_ja: CI/CDパイプラインを設計・実装
description_en: Design and implement a CI/CD pipeline
content: Design a CI/CD pipeline for [PROJECT TYPE]. Language/Framework: [STACK]. Repository: [GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket]. Deployment target: [TARGET - AWS/GCP/Azure/Vercel/DigitalOcean]. Team workflow: [GITFLOW/TRUNK-BASED/FEATURE BRANCHES]. Create: 1) Pipeline stages overview (trigger → test → build → deploy), 2) GitHub Actions / GitLab CI YAML configuration, 3) Test automation stage (unit + integration tests), 4) Code quality gates (linting, coverage thresholds, security scanning), 5) Build and containerization steps, 6) Environment-specific deployment configs (dev/staging/prod), 7) Secrets management approach, 8) Rollback strategy, 9) Notification setup (Slack/email on failure), 10) Pipeline optimization tips (caching, parallelization). Include estimated pipeline execution time and cost estimates for common CI providers.

title: Database Schema Designer
description_ja: データベーススキーマを設計
description_en: Design an optimized database schema
content: Design an optimized database schema for [APPLICATION TYPE]. Database: [MySQL/PostgreSQL/MongoDB]. Scale: [EXPECTED DATA VOLUME]. Key features: [LIST MAIN FEATURES]. Create: 1) Entity list with attributes (all tables/collections needed), 2) Relationship diagram description (foreign keys, cardinality), 3) Full SQL/schema definition with data types and constraints, 4) Index strategy (which columns to index and why), 5) Normalization analysis (what normal form and trade-offs), 6) Denormalization recommendations for performance-critical queries, 7) Soft delete implementation, 8) Audit trail / created_at / updated_at standards, 9) Sample queries for the 5 most common operations, 10) Migration strategy (how to safely apply schema changes in production). Also flag: what will be slow at scale and how to address it.

title: Error Handling Pattern Guide
description_ja: エラーハンドリングのパターンを設計
description_en: Implement robust error handling patterns
content: Design a robust error handling system for [APPLICATION TYPE] in [LANGUAGE/FRAMEWORK]. Current code or description: [PASTE OR DESCRIBE]. Create: 1) Error taxonomy (define error types: operational vs programmer errors, expected vs unexpected), 2) Custom error classes hierarchy with code examples, 3) Error propagation strategy (how errors bubble up through layers), 4) HTTP error response format standard (for APIs), 5) Client-facing vs. developer-facing error messages (what to show where), 6) Centralized error handling middleware/handler, 7) Logging strategy (what to log, structured format, correlation IDs), 8) Retry logic for transient failures, 9) Circuit breaker pattern implementation, 10) Error monitoring and alerting setup recommendations. Provide before/after code examples showing the improvement. Include an anti-patterns section (common error handling mistakes to avoid).

title: Docker Setup Generator
description_ja: Dockerの設定ファイルを自動生成
description_en: Generate a production-ready Docker setup
content: Generate a production-ready Docker setup for [APPLICATION TYPE] built with [TECH STACK]. Environment: [DEVELOPMENT/PRODUCTION/BOTH]. Create: 1) Optimized Dockerfile (multi-stage build for minimal image size), 2) .dockerignore file, 3) Docker Compose for local development (app + all required services: database/cache/queue), 4) Docker Compose override for production differences, 5) Environment variable handling (build args vs runtime env), 6) Health check configuration, 7) Volume mounts strategy (dev hot reload vs prod), 8) Network configuration (service isolation), 9) Resource limits recommendations, 10) Makefile or shell script for common commands (build/run/stop/logs/shell). Explain each Dockerfile instruction and why it's ordered that way. Include image size optimization tips and security best practices (non-root user, minimal base image, etc.).

title: Technical Interview Prep Kit
description_ja: 技術面接の準備キットを作成
description_en: Prepare a technical interview prep kit
content: Create a technical interview preparation kit for a [ROLE - e.g., frontend/backend/fullstack/data engineer] position at a [COMPANY TYPE - startup/FAANG/enterprise]. Candidate background: [YEARS EXP + STACK]. Create: 1) Core concept checklist (must-know topics for this role with confidence rating system), 2) Top 20 likely technical questions with ideal answer frameworks, 3) System design question walkthrough (step-by-step approach for common scenarios), 4) Algorithm and data structure cheat sheet (most relevant for this role), 5) Behavioral question prep (STAR format for 5 common scenarios), 6) Code review exercise (common patterns to spot), 7) Questions to ask the interviewer (10 smart questions), 8) Live coding tips (how to think out loud effectively), 9) Red flags to avoid in technical discussions, 10) 1-week study plan leading up to the interview.
